test_plan.strategy: Unit tests cover collector logic (readSpecArtifacts, graphNormalizer) and collapse handler. Integration tests verify end-to-end data flow from collector to dashboard.json. E2E tests (Playwright) verify Graph tab rendering, interaction flows, and responsive layout.

test_cases.title: aitri-hub monitor prints redirect message and exits 0
preconditions: lib/commands/monitor.js is deleted
monitor stub is present in bin/aitri-hub.js dispatch
test_cases.given: aitri-hub CLI is installed with the monitor stub in place
test_cases.when: the command `aitri-hub monitor` is executed in a shell
test_cases.then: stdout contains "monitor removed — run 'aitri-hub web' to open the dashboard" and process exits with code 0
test_cases.expected_result: Exit code 0; stdout includes redirect message

test_cases.title: aitri-hub --help does not list monitor as a valid command
preconditions: lib/commands/monitor.js is deleted
test_cases.given: aitri-hub CLI is installed with the monitor stub in place
test_cases.when: `aitri-hub --help` is executed
test_cases.then: stdout does not contain the substring 'monitor' in the commands listing; 'setup' and 'web' are both present in stdout
test_cases.expected_result: No 'monitor' in help output; 'setup' and 'web' present

test_cases.title: aitri-hub setup completes successfully after monitor and cli.js removal
preconditions: lib/commands/monitor.js deleted
lib/renderer/cli.js deleted
temp hub dir at os.tmpdir()/aitri-hub-test-setup/
test_cases.given: aitri-hub CLI installed with monitor and cli.js removed; no projects.json at temp hub dir
test_cases.when: `aitri-hub setup` is executed with pre-answered prompts supplying location=os.tmpdir()
test_cases.then: process exits with code 0; projects.json is created at temp hub dir containing one entry with location equal to os.tmpdir()
steps: Run: aitri-hub setup with piped answers
test_cases.expected_result: Exit code 0; projects.json exists with 1 entry

test_cases.title: All 7 tabs including Graph visible at 768px without horizontal scroll
preconditions: aitri-hub web running at localhost:3000
1 project registered in dashboard.json
test_cases.given: aitri-hub web is running; dashboard.json has 1 project
test_cases.when: Playwright opens http://localhost:3000 with viewport 768x1024
test_cases.then: tab bar contains exactly 7 tab buttons; button with text 'Graph' is visible; tab bar scrollWidth === tab bar clientWidth (no overflow)
test_cases.expected_result: 7 tabs, 'Graph' visible, no horizontal scroll

test_cases.title: Graph tab panel renders at 375px mobile viewport without overflow
preconditions: aitri-hub web running at localhost:3000
1 project registered
test_cases.given: aitri-hub web running; 1 project with specArtifacts.status=loaded
test_cases.when: Playwright opens http://localhost:3000 at viewport 375x667 and clicks the 'Graph' tab
test_cases.then: Graph tab panel is visible; document.documentElement.scrollWidth === 375 (no horizontal overflow); project selector width ≤ 375px
test_cases.expected_result: No overflow at 375px; Graph panel visible

test_cases.title: Clicking Graph tab does not trigger a full page reload
preconditions: aitri-hub web running at localhost:3000; browser on Overview tab
test_cases.given: browser is on the Overview tab at http://localhost:3000
test_cases.when: Playwright attaches a 'load' event listener to window then clicks the 'Graph' tab button
test_cases.then: the 'load' event is NOT fired; Graph tab panel becomes visible within 500ms without a navigation event
test_cases.expected_result: No page reload; Graph panel visible within 500ms

test_cases.title: readSpecArtifacts returns loaded with correct FR and TC counts
preconditions: temp dir at os.tmpdir()/aitri-spec-test/ with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json (10 FRs) and spec/03_TEST_CASES.json (20 TCs)
test_cases.given: temp project dir at os.tmpdir()/aitri-spec-test/ with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing 10 functional_requirements and spec/03_TEST_CASES.json containing 20 test_cases
test_cases.when: readSpecArtifacts({ location: os.tmpdir()+'/aitri-spec-test', name: 'test' }) is called
test_cases.then: returns { status: 'loaded', requirements: { functional_requirements: Array(10) }, testCases: { test_cases: Array(20) }, error: null }
test_cases.expected_result: status=loaded; 10 FRs; 20 TCs; error=null

test_cases.title: readSpecArtifacts returns loaded with null testCases when 03_TEST_CASES.json absent
preconditions: temp dir at os.tmpdir()/aitri-req-only/ with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json (5 FRs); no 03_TEST_CASES.json
test_cases.given: temp dir with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json (5 FRs) and no spec/03_TEST_CASES.json
test_cases.when: readSpecArtifacts({ location: os.tmpdir()+'/aitri-req-only', name: 'test' }) is called
test_cases.then: returns { status: 'loaded', requirements: { functional_requirements: Array(5) }, testCases: null, error: null }
steps: Create temp dir with only requirements file
test_cases.expected_result: status=loaded; 5 FRs; testCases=null

test_cases.title: readSpecArtifacts returns error status for malformed JSON without throwing
preconditions: temp dir at os.tmpdir()/aitri-bad-json/ with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing '{invalid json::'
test_cases.given: temp dir with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing the string '{invalid json::'
test_cases.when: readSpecArtifacts({ location: os.tmpdir()+'/aitri-bad-json', name: 'test' }) is called
test_cases.then: function does NOT throw; returns { status: 'error', requirements: null, testCases: null, error: <string containing 'JSON'> }
steps: Assert no throw and correct return
test_cases.expected_result: No throw; status=error; error message contains 'JSON'

test_cases.title: readSpecArtifacts returns remote status for GitHub URL without filesystem access
test_cases.given: a project entry with location='https://github.com/owner/repo'
test_cases.when: readSpecArtifacts({ location: 'https://github.com/owner/repo', name: 'test' }) is called
test_cases.then: returns { status: 'remote', requirements: null, testCases: null, error: null }; no fs.readFile or fs.readFileSync call is made
steps: Call readSpecArtifacts with a GitHub URL
Assert status=remote and no fs calls
test_cases.expected_result: status=remote; null fields; no filesystem access

test_cases.title: readSpecArtifacts strips description and acceptance_criteria from FR entries
preconditions: temp dir with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json where each FR has id, title, priority, type, description (500 chars), acceptance_criteria (array of 5 strings)
test_cases.given: spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json where FR entries contain fields: id, title, priority, type, description='long text...', acceptance_criteria=['AC1','AC2','AC3','AC4','AC5']
test_cases.when: readSpecArtifacts is called and result.requirements is JSON.stringified
test_cases.then: the serialized output contains no 'description' key and no 'acceptance_criteria' key at any FR entry; each FR has exactly the keys: id, title, priority, type
test_cases.expected_result: No description or acceptance_criteria keys in output FRs

test_cases.title: collectAll includes specArtifacts in dashboard.json for local project
preconditions: temp project at os.tmpdir()/aitri-int-test/ with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json (3 FRs) and spec/03_TEST_CASES.json (6 TCs)
test_cases.given: projects.json registers one project at os.tmpdir()/aitri-int-test/ with valid spec files (3 FRs, 6 TCs); hub data dir set to temp location
test_cases.when: runCollectionCycle() completes and dashboard.json is read from hub data dir
test_cases.then: projects[0].specArtifacts.status === 'loaded'; projects[0].specArtifacts.requirements.functional_requirements.length === 3; projects[0].specArtifacts.testCases.test_cases.length === 6
steps: Set up temp project and hub dir
test_cases.expected_result: specArtifacts in dashboard.json with status=loaded, 3 FRs, 6 TCs

test_cases.title: normalizeSpecArtifacts produces FR nodes (type fr) and TC nodes (type tc) with edges
test_cases.given: specArtifacts with requirements containing FR-001 and FR-002; testCases containing TC-001 (requirement_id:'FR-001'), TC-002 (requirement_id:'FR-001'), TC-003 (requirement_id:'FR-002')
test_cases.when: normalizeSpecArtifacts(specArtifacts) is called
test_cases.then: returns { nodes: Array(5), edges: Array(3) }; nodes include { data: { id:'FR-001', type:'fr' } } and { data: { id:'TC-001', type:'tc', parentFr:'FR-001' } }; edges include { data: { source:'FR-001', target:'TC-001' } }
test_cases.expected_result: 5 nodes (2 FR + 3 TC); 3 edges; correct type and parentFr fields

test_cases.title: Graph renders 50 FRs + 150 TCs within 2000ms of project selection
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; project registered with specArtifacts containing 50 FRs and 150 TCs
test_cases.given: aitri-hub web running; dashboard.json project has specArtifacts.status=loaded with 50 FRs and 150 TCs with valid requirement_id links
test_cases.when: Playwright selects that project in the Graph tab dropdown; performance.now() recorded at selection and at layoutstop event
test_cases.then: elapsed time from dropdown change to layoutstop ≤ 2000ms
test_cases.expected_result: Layout completes in ≤ 2000ms for 200 nodes

test_cases.title: normalizeSpecArtifacts returns empty nodes and edges for non-loaded specArtifacts
test_cases.given: specArtifacts with status='missing' (requirements=null, testCases=null)
test_cases.when: normalizeSpecArtifacts({ status: 'missing', requirements: null, testCases: null }) is called
test_cases.then: returns { nodes: [], edges: [] } — no throw, no nodes produced
test_cases.expected_result: { nodes: [], edges: [] } — no crash

test_cases.title: normalizeSpecArtifacts assigns approved status to FR in approved phase
test_cases.given: specArtifacts with aitriState.approvedPhases=[1,2,3]; requirements containing FR-001 of type logic (in phase 1)
test_cases.when: normalizeSpecArtifacts(specArtifacts) is called
test_cases.then: node with id='FR-001' has data.status === 'approved'
test_cases.expected_result: FR-001 node status=approved

test_cases.title: normalizeSpecArtifacts assigns drift status to approved FRs when project hasDrift is true
test_cases.given: project aitriState where driftPhases=['1'] and approvedPhases=[1]; specArtifacts.requirements contains FR-003
test_cases.when: normalizeSpecArtifacts(specArtifacts, aitriState) is called with aitriState={ driftPhases: ['1'], approvedPhases: [1], completedPhases: [1], currentPhase: 1 }
test_cases.then: node with id='FR-003' has data.status === 'drift' (driftPhases.map(String).includes('1') is true)
test_cases.expected_result: FR-003 node status=drift

test_cases.title: Legend panel is visible and contains exactly 4 status entries when graph is loaded
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; Graph tab active with loaded project
test_cases.given: aitri-hub web running; Graph tab open with specArtifacts.status=loaded
test_cases.when: Playwright queries the legend component in the DOM
test_cases.then: legend element is visible (not display:none); it contains exactly 4 child items with text labels: 'approved', 'in_progress', 'pending', 'drift'
test_cases.expected_result: Legend visible with 4 labelled entries

test_cases.title: Clicking FR node hides its 8 TC children and shows badge (8)
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; Graph tab open with project where FR-005 has 8 linked TCs (TC-101 through TC-108)
test_cases.given: Graph tab open; FR-005 has 8 children TC-101–TC-108 all visible in Cytoscape canvas
test_cases.when: Playwright clicks the Cytoscape node with data-id='FR-005'
test_cases.then: cy.getElementById('TC-101').removed() === true (and same for TC-102 through TC-108); FR-005 node label contains '(8)'
steps: Open Graph tab with test project
Assert TC nodes removed and badge present
test_cases.expected_result: 8 TC nodes removed from graph; FR-005 label shows '(8)'

test_cases.title: Clicking collapsed FR node restores children within 200ms
preconditions: Graph tab open; FR-005 already collapsed with badge (8)
test_cases.given: FR-005 is collapsed (8 TC children hidden, badge '(8)' visible in label)
test_cases.when: Playwright clicks FR-005 node; performance.now() recorded before click and after cy.getElementById('TC-101').removed() === false
test_cases.then: elapsed time ≤ 200ms; cy.getElementById('TC-101').removed() === false; FR-005 label does NOT contain '(8)'
test_cases.expected_result: Children restored ≤ 200ms; badge removed from label

test_cases.title: Collapse handler does nothing for FR with no linked TCs
test_cases.given: collapseMap is empty (new Map()); Cytoscape collection has FR-007 with no TC children (no edges from FR-007)
test_cases.when: the collapse handler is called with nodeId='FR-007' and the current cy collection
test_cases.then: collapseMap.has('FR-007') === false; no cy.remove() is called; FR-007 node label is unchanged
steps: Build minimal cy fixture with FR-007 and no TC children
test_cases.expected_result: collapseMap unchanged; no nodes removed; label unchanged

test_cases.title: Project selector shows all 3 registered projects within 500ms of tab activation
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; dashboard.json has 3 projects: alpha, beta, gamma
test_cases.given: dashboard.json contains 3 projects with names 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma'; browser is on Overview tab
test_cases.when: Playwright clicks the 'Graph' tab; performance.now() recorded at click and when dropdown options are present in DOM
test_cases.then: dropdown contains exactly 3 options with text 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma'; time from tab click to options visible ≤ 500ms
test_cases.expected_result: 3 dropdown options; populated within 500ms

test_cases.title: Single project is auto-selected and graph renders without user interaction
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; dashboard.json has exactly 1 project 'solo-project' with specArtifacts.status=loaded
test_cases.given: dashboard.json contains exactly 1 project named 'solo-project' with specArtifacts.status=loaded and 5 FRs
test_cases.when: Playwright clicks the 'Graph' tab and waits for layoutstop event
test_cases.then: project selector shows 'solo-project' as selected value; cy.nodes().length > 0 (graph is not empty); no user interaction with dropdown was needed
steps: Assert selector value and non-empty graph
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-selected 'solo-project'; graph rendered without dropdown interaction

test_cases.title: Graph tab shows setup guidance when 0 projects registered
preconditions: aitri-hub web running; dashboard.json has projects: []
test_cases.given: dashboard.json contains an empty projects array []
test_cases.when: Playwright opens http://localhost:3000 and clicks the 'Graph' tab
test_cases.then: the project selector element is absent or has display:none; canvas area contains visible text including the string 'aitri-hub setup'
steps: Open Graph tab with empty projects
test_cases.expected_result: No selector; canvas shows 'aitri-hub setup' guidance
